title: "Constantinople’s Relic Puzzle v108"

  description: "In a cloistered hall of Constantinople’s Great Basilica, a sacred relic disappeared at precisely 21:40. Four individuals (1, 2, 3, 4) were recorded inside shortly before the relic vanished. Exactly one suspect lies in all their statements; the other three speak only truths. Only one suspect is truly guilty. Each suspect now provides five statements, weaving claims about each other’s presence and timeline:

    Suspect 1:
      (1) 'If 2 is telling the truth, then 4 left the basilica by 21:35.'
      (2) 'I exited at 21:30, so I was gone before the relic vanished at 21:40.'
      (3) '3 was still wandering the inner corridor at 21:35.'
      (4) 'Either 2 never approached the relic after dusk, or 3 is misleading us—but never both.'
      (5) 'If 4 was actually absent before the final chant, then 2 must be lying.'

    Suspect 2:
      (1) '1 actually remained until at least 21:45, contradicting the 21:30 claim.'
      (2) 'I did not even pass by the relic after 21:20, so I cannot be guilty.'
      (3) 'If 3 is lying, then 4’s entire account is correct.'
      (4) 'Either 1’s timeline is false, or 3 is guilty—but not both at once.'
      (5) 'If 1 is truthful about leaving early, then 4 cannot have vanished so soon.'

    Suspect 3:
      (1) 'I did not set foot near the relic after 21:15.'
      (2) 'If 4 speaks truth, then 2’s second statement must be false.'
      (3) 'Either 2 is lying, or 1 departed after 21:30—never both.'
      (4) '1 was still inside past 21:35, which contradicts the claim of leaving at 21:30.'
      (5) 'I am not the culprit; records place me in the outer courtyard by the final bell.'

    Suspect 4:
      (1) '3 was definitely near the relic around 21:35, contradicting 3’s denial.'
      (2) 'If 1 is lying about the 21:30 exit, then 2 must be wholly truthful.'
      (3) 'I am not guilty; the watchers saw me outside at 21:40.'
      (4) 'Either 2 lingered near the relic beyond 21:20, or 3 is entirely deceptive—but not both.'
      (5) 'If 3 is telling truth, then 1’s timeline is entirely false.'

  question: "Which suspect (1, 2, 3, or 4) stole the relic? Return only one digit. Exactly one scenario remains consistent."
answer: >
  explanation: "Analytical Breakdown:

  Let L(x) = 'x lies in all statements' and T(x) = 'x tells only truths,' G(x) = 'x is guilty.'
  We know exactly one L(x) is true and one G(x) is true; the others T(x)=true.

  Case L(1):
    Then 1’s (1)–(5) are false. 1 did not exit at 21:30 => possible presence at 21:40 => suspicious. But 2,3,4 being truthful triggers mutual contradictions (like 3(4) vs 4(1)). Inconsistency arises.

  Case L(2):
    Then 2’s (1)–(5) are false => 2 might have approached after 21:20 => suspicious. 1,3,4 are truthful => times or blame soon clash between 1(3) '3 was inside 21:35' and 3(5) 'outer courtyard by final bell' if both are T. Contradiction emerges.

  Case L(3):
    Then 3’s (1)–(5) are false => 3 must have approached the relic after 21:15 => possibility of guilt. Meanwhile 1,2,4 are truthful => no direct contradictions remain: 1(2) 'Left 21:30 => not culprit,' 2(2) 'didn't pass relic after 21:20 => not culprit,' 4(3) 'I am not guilty => watchers saw me outside 21:40 => not culprit.' Hence G(3)=true. Consistent.

  Case L(4):
    Then 4’s (1)–(5) are false => 1,2,3 truthful => times produce direct conflict with 3(4) '1 inside past 21:35' if 1(2) stands. Contradictions arise.

  Only L(3) => G(3) stands with no conflict. Culprit is suspect 3.

  final: '3'"
